info.name: New Starter Kit API
info.description:
Complete API collection for the New Starter Kit authentication system.

Base URL: {{base_url}}{{api_prefix}}

## Authentication
- Access Token: Returned in login/refresh response body. Set automatically via test scripts.
- Refresh Token: HTTP-only cookie set automatically by the server.

## Quick Start
1. Import this collection and the environment file
2. Select 'New Starter Kit - Local' environment
3. Run 'Register' then 'Verify Email' then 'Login'
4. All authenticated requests will use the saved access token automatically

item.name: 🔐 Authentication
item.description:
Complete authentication lifecycle — register, verify, login, maintain sessions, and sign out.
🔄 Typical flow:

Register → 2. Verify Email → 3. Login → 4. Use access_token → 5. Refresh → 6. Logout

💡 After Login, the access_token environment variable is set automatically by the test script.
The refresh_token is stored as an HTTP-only cookie managed by Postman's cookie jar.

request.description:
Create a new user account. On success (201), an email verification code is dispatched to
the provided address. The account cannot log in until the email is verified.
Response data includes: user (uuid, email, firstname, lastname, isVerified) and emailSent flag.

exec: // Save access token to environment for subsequent requests
// Save temp token if 2FA is required
pm.test("Refresh token cookie is set", function () {

request.description:
Authenticate with email and password. On success:

Returns an access token in the response body (saved automatically to {{access_token}})
Sets an HTTP-only refresh_token cookie (7-day or session TTL based on rememberMe)

The account must be email-verified before login is permitted.

Invalidates the current session by revoking the active refresh token and clearing the
HTTP-only cookie. Requires a valid Bearer access token.
The test script automatically clears {{access_token}} from the environment.

Revokes ALL active refresh tokens for the authenticated user across every device and browser.
Use this for security incidents or account takeover scenarios.
The test script automatically clears {{access_token}} from the environment.

exec: pm.test("Response has new access token", function () {

request.description:
Exchanges the current HTTP-only refresh_token cookie for a new access token.
Implements refresh token rotation: the old token is immediately revoked and a new
cookie is issued. Uses cookie-based auth — no Authorization header required.
The test script updates {{access_token}} automatically.

item.name: � Two-Factor Authentication
item.description:
Two-factor authentication flow for additional account security.
When a user with 2FA enabled logs in, they receive a temporary token and must verify
their identity with a 6-digit OTP sent to their email.

request.description: Complete 2FA login verification. Requires the 6-digit OTP code from email and the temporary JWT from the login response. On success, returns access token and sets refresh_token cookie.
Resend a new 6-digit OTP code during the 2FA login flow. Requires the temporary JWT from the initial login response.

item.name: � Email Verification
item.description:
Email address verification flow using a one-time 6-digit code.
The verification code is sent on registration and can be resent if expired.
Set the {{verification_token}} environment variable with the code from your inbox
before running Verify Email.

request.description:
Submit the 6-digit OTP received by email to mark the account as verified.
The body field name is "token" (not "code" or "otp").
Prerequisite: Set {{verification_token}} in the environment with the code from your email.

Request a new verification code if the original expired or was not received.
Rate limited to prevent abuse. Always returns 200 — anti-enumeration by design.

item.name: 🔓 Password Recovery
item.description:
Two-step password reset flow: request a reset link via email, then submit a new password
using the token from that email.
Set {{reset_token}} from your inbox before running Reset Password.
Anti-enumeration: Forgot Password always returns 200 regardless of whether the email exists.

request.description:
Initiates the password reset flow by sending a reset link to the specified email.
Security note: Always returns 200 OK regardless of whether the email is registered.
This prevents user enumeration attacks. Check your inbox for the reset token.

Completes the password reset using the token from the reset email.
All active sessions for the user are revoked on success (security wipe).
Prerequisite: Set {{reset_token}} in the environment with the token from your reset email.
Password must meet complexity requirements (min 8 chars, uppercase, lowercase, number, special char).

item.name: 👤 User
item.description:
Authenticated user profile endpoints. All requests require a valid Bearer access token.
Run Login first to populate {{access_token}}.

request.description:
Returns the authenticated user's profile data: uuid, email, firstname, lastname,
role, isVerified, createdAt, and updatedAt.
Requires: Bearer {{access_token}} in Authorization header.
Update the authenticated user's first name and last name. Both fields must be 3-16 characters, letters and spaces only.
Upload a profile avatar image. Accepts JPEG, PNG, WebP, or GIF. File is uploaded to Cloudinary.

item.name: 📧 Email Management
item.description:
Email address management for authenticated users.
Request an email change and confirm it via a verification link sent to the new address.

request.description: Request an email address change. Sends a verification link to the new email. Requires current password for security.

Confirm an email address change via the token from the verification email. This is a browser-navigated endpoint that returns 302 redirects to the frontend. Normally accessed by clicking the link in the verification email.

item.name: 🔒 Account Security
item.description:
Account security management endpoints for authenticated users.
Change password and toggle two-factor authentication.

request.description: Change the authenticated user's password. Requires current password and a new password meeting complexity requirements (8-128 chars, uppercase, lowercase, number, special character).
Enable or disable two-factor authentication. Set 'enable' to true/false. Requires current password for security.

item.name: 🏥 Health
item.description:
System health and liveness check. No authentication required.
Use this to verify the server is running before starting an API testing session.

item.name: 🛡️ Security & Test Endpoints
item.description:
Diagnostic and security validation endpoints exposed by the test router.
These routes are NOT part of the production API surface — they exist solely to verify
middleware behavior: error handling, XSS sanitization, helmet headers, and security stats.
⚠️ These endpoints are only available when NODE_ENV=development or NODE_ENV=test.
They are disabled in production builds.
Base path: {{base_url}}/test  (no api_prefix — test router is mounted separately)

item.name: 💥 Test Error Handling
item.description:
Trigger each class of error response to verify the global error handler middleware
returns the correct status code, envelope shape, and errorCode.
All responses should follow the standard error envelope:
{ success: false, message: "...", errorCode: "...", timestamp: "..." }

exec: pm.test("No stack trace in response", function () {

request.description:
Triggers an unhandled generic server error to verify the global error handler
returns a 500 with the standard error envelope. Confirms no stack traces leak to the client.

Triggers a simulated express-validator validation failure to confirm the validation
middleware returns 400 with a structured error array in the response body.

Simulates a database connection or query failure to verify the error handler
maps DB errors to the correct HTTP status and does not expose connection strings
or internal query details in the response.

Triggers a simulated 401 authentication error to verify the error handler returns
the correct status code and error envelope without leaking token internals.

exec: pm.test("Response is JSON not HTML", function () {

request.description:
Hits a route that does not exist to verify the 404 not-found middleware fires
and returns the standard error envelope with a 404 status — not an HTML page.

item.name: 🧹 Sanitize Middleware
item.description:
Verify that the sanitization middleware strips XSS payloads from all input vectors:
query parameters, URL params, and JSON request bodies.
Each request contains deliberate XSS attack strings. The sanitized values should
appear in the response with all dangerous HTML/JS removed or escaped.

request.description:
Sends XSS payloads in query parameters. The response should reflect the sanitized
(stripped) versions of each value — all script tags, event handlers, and dangerous
attributes removed. Validates that GET query params pass through the sanitize middleware.

body.raw:
{ "username": "user123", "email": "test@example.com", "bio": "I love and ", "htmlContent": "
Dangerous
", "link": "Click me" }

request.description:
Sends XSS payloads in a JSON request body across multiple field types.
The response should reflect sanitized field values — iframe tags, script tags,
and javascript: href schemes must be stripped. Validates body sanitization.

Tests sanitization of URL path parameters combined with malicious query strings.
Verifies that the middleware handles both route params and query strings simultaneously.

item.name: ☠️ Dangerous Input Test
item.description:
Advanced XSS attack vector tests including cookie-stealing payloads, form injection,
event handler injection, javascript: URL schemes, and iframe phishing attacks.
These are real-world attack patterns. The API must neutralize all of them before
the data reaches any business logic layer.

exec: pm.test("All dangerous payloads are neutralized", function () {

body.raw:
{ "xssPayload": "", "htmlInjection": "
", "eventHandlers": "", "javascriptUrl": "Safe Link", "iframeAttack": "" }

request.description:
Submits a comprehensive set of advanced attack payloads targeting cookie theft,
credential phishing via form injection, API exfiltration via onerror handlers,
javascript: URL scheme abuse, and iframe-based phishing.
Expected: All payloads stripped/escaped. No dangerous HTML survives to the response.

Tests that GET query parameters containing script injection are sanitized correctly.
Simpler than the POST variant but confirms the middleware covers all HTTP methods.

item.name: 🔬 Comprehensive Security Test
item.description:
End-to-end security middleware validation: sanitization of nested objects and arrays,
helmet security header verification, rate limiter statistics, and combined
query-string + body attack coverage.
Run these requests after the individual sanitize tests to confirm holistic middleware
behavior across all input surfaces simultaneously.

exec: pm.test("All vectors are sanitized including nested", function () {

request.description:
The most thorough sanitization test. Combines malicious query params with a body
containing safe fields, XSS fields, mixed content (safe HTML + malicious tags),
nested object fields, and arrays containing attack strings.
Verifies that the sanitizer recursively processes all input shapes — not just flat fields.

exec: pm.test("XSS query param is sanitized", function () {

request.description:
GET variant of the comprehensive security test. Confirms that the sanitizer handles
mixed safe/malicious query parameters and does not corrupt legitimate values.

Verifies that the Helmet middleware is applying all required security headers.
Check the Response Headers tab for: Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options,
X-Content-Type-Options, Strict-Transport-Security, and Referrer-Policy.

exec: pm.test("Stats response is valid JSON", function () {

request.description:
Returns aggregated security diagnostic statistics: request counts, sanitization
hit rates, rate limiter counters, and any flagged payloads from recent requests.
Use this after running the other security tests to see aggregate sanitizer activity.
